acceptance_criteria: telemetry_limit_checks.json contains final_decision_table and explicit range_inclusive flags.
In-limit samples have non-zero deviation_from_nearest_limit values (distance to nearest bound).
rationale.md references the JSON keys and documents that full-file verification was performed.
Deterministic precheck and verifier return pass/warning-free status or explicitly document remaining concerns.
extra_model_fields.priority_reasoning: Changes are small and targeted at clarity and verifier concern; they are low-effort but improve reproducibility and remove an unsupported claim.

verification_steps: Open full artifact files and compare to previews.
Validate that telemetry_limit_checks.json is valid JSON and includes final_decision_table and limit.range_inclusive keys.
Run deterministic precheck to ensure artifact_contract still passes.
Run model grader and verifier; confirm verifier no longer flags unsupported semantic claims or update grader language accordingly.

planned_changes: Open the full artifact files (telemetry_limit_checks.json and rationale.md) and verify that the truncated preview portions match the rest of the content; record any discrepancies.

If not already present, add a top-level 'final_decision_table' array to telemetry_limit_checks.json containing one machine-readable object per sample with fields: packet_row, sample_index, channel, value, unit, limit, result, severity, deviation_from_nearest_limit, deviation_pct_of_violated_boundary, citation, recommended_action.
Add explicit inclusivity metadata to each limit object in telemetry_limit_checks.json (e.g., limit.range_inclusive: true/false) and mirror that information in rationale.md.

For every in-limit reading, compute and set deviation_from_nearest_limit to the absolute distance to the nearest boundary (not zero). Keep deviation_pct_of_violated_boundary as 0 for in-limit samples but ensure the field exists.

Update rationale.md to: reference the new JSON keys (final_decision_table and limit.range_inclusive), show the short verification checklist confirming full-file consistency, and remove/qualify any unverified claims of full semantic correctness until post-revision verification is performed.

Run the deterministic precheck and model grader/verifier flows locally or via CI to obtain updated grader_result and verifier_result; ensure grader statements accurately reflect whether full-file semantic verification was completed.
Submit revised attempt 117-revised and include a short note in final_message.txt summarizing the checks performed and the changes made.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address verifier warning about unsupported semantic-correctness claim based on truncated previews and implement small machine-readability and diagnostic improvements to telemetry_limit_checks.json and rationale.md.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Artifacts satisfy the deliverable contract and visible preview content is consistent and correct. The verifier flagged a procedural weakness: the model grader asserted full semantic correctness despite previews being truncated. Also, there are small, actionable improvements to improve machine-readability and diagnostic usefulness (explicit machine-readable final_decision_table, indicate range inclusivity in JSON, and report non-zero deviation_from_nearest_limit for in-limit samples).
